name: web-build-deploy
description: Building and deploying React web applications. Use when configuring builds, deploying to Vercel/Netlify, setting up CI/CD, Docker, or managing environments.
capabilities: Deploy React apps to Vercel with vercel.json configuration
Deploy React apps to Netlify with netlify.toml configuration
Build Docker containers with multi-stage Dockerfile
Set up environment variables for build tools
Implement security headers and caching strategies
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown reference material for React deployment. No executable code, file access, network calls, or external commands. Contains only example code snippets demonstrating deployment workflows. All static findings are false positives - the scanner misinterpreted documentation examples as executable code patterns.
valueStatement: Need to deploy a React application but unsure about the best platform and configuration. This skill provides ready-to-use deployment patterns for Vercel, Netlify, Docker, and CI/CD pipelines with security best practices included.
userTitle: Deploy React Apps to Vercel, Netlify, and Docker
limitations: Does not execute deployment commands automatically
Does not create or modify project files
Does not access cloud provider credentials
Requires manual execution of example commands

useCases.title: Deploy first React app
useCases.description: Choose platform, configure build, and deploy React application with proper security headers

useCases.title: Set up CI/CD pipeline
useCases.description: Configure GitHub Actions workflow with automated testing and deployment to production

useCases.title: Containerize React app
useCases.description: Create optimized Docker image with multi-stage build and nginx serving

promptTemplates.title: Basic Vercel deploy
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I deploy my React app to Vercel? Show me the vercel.json configuration with security headers.

promptTemplates.title: Netlify deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the commands to deploy to Netlify? Include the netlify.toml configuration file.

promptTemplates.title: Docker production
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me a production-ready Dockerfile for my React app with multi-stage build and nginx.

promptTemplates.title: CI/CD workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds, tests, and deploys to Vercel on push to main branch.

bestPractices: Always configure security headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options) in deployment config
Use environment variables with proper prefixes (VITE_, NEXT_PUBLIC_) for client-side access
Enable caching for static assets to improve performance
antiPatterns: Committing sensitive credentials to version control
Using environment variable names without proper prefixes for client-side access
Deploying without health check endpoints or error tracking

faq.question: Which platform should I choose for my React app?
faq.answer: Vercel offers best integration for Next.js. Netlify works well for any static site. Choose based on your framework and existing tooling.

faq.question: What environment variables do I need for build?
faq.answer: Use VITE_ prefix for Vite apps, NEXT_PUBLIC_ for Next.js. Server-only variables should not have these prefixes.

faq.question: How do I configure SPA routing on production?
faq.answer: Configure server fallback to index.html for all routes. Both Vercel and Netlify support this via rewrites configuration.

faq.question: Is my code safe when deploying?
faq.answer: This skill only provides documentation examples. You execute deployment commands yourself. Never commit secrets to version control.

faq.question: Build fails on CI but works locally?
faq.answer: Check Node version matches local environment. Clear CI cache and verify environment variables are set in CI secrets.

faq.question: How is this different from other deployment tools?
faq.answer: This is a reference guide, not an execution tool. It shows configuration patterns and commands for manual or scripted deployment.
